Peloton已经提出了一个有趣的解决方案，可以解决其与孩子的死亡有关的跑步机：4,295美元的扶手+的强制所有者，以便退还机器退款，或支付39美元的月度会员费，以便使用它。用户愤怒，有些甚至将Peloton对赎金软件的需求进行了比较。
 这是一个提醒，当您购买或使用其访问由其他人控制的产品时，可以随时从您身上带走。随着我们购买的越来越多的事情，我们购买的互联网，由制造商推动的软件更新和可以随时快门的平台的生活和死亡，我们对它们的控制较少和更少。即使我们为这些设备支付了相当数量的钱，我们也可能完全拥有它们。
 在这种情况下，Peloton表示出于安全原因制造的变化，并将客户迫使客户进入成员资格是实施更新的唯一方法。 Peloton在5月份召回胎面+，后来在跑步机下拉的孩子死亡之后;对其他其他儿童，宠物和成年人伤害的报告;和消费产品安全委员会（CPSC）的公共吐痰。该公司停止销售胎面+并敦促胎面+业主将其“立即停止”，并为11月之前退回的设备提供全额退款。但Peloton并没有完全阻止人们使用机器。事实上，它甚至可以向所有者家庭发送搬运工，以便免费将其胎面+单位重新安置到孩子和宠物无法访问的空间。
 现在，一些胎面+所有者将面临额外的每月收费，他们以前没有。 Peloton最近通知他们很快他们将不再能够使用他们的机器，除非他们每月39美元购买全权收购成员。 Peloton提供三个月的全程免费弥补给您带来的不便。
 Peloton告诉Recode的举措是必要的，为机器添加名为“胎面锁”的安全功能。
 “在我们正在进行的工作中，确保我们的产品甚至更安全，并且作为我们与CPSC合作的自愿召回的一部分，我们发布了一个四位数的密码来保护胎面+以防止未经授权的访问，”Peloton告诉Recode。 “遗憾的是，由于目前的技术限制，没有Peloton会员的胎面锁尚未提供。”
 Peloton告诉Recode，它正在努力使胎面锁免于免费，但不会说实现更改需要多长时间。如果需要不到三个月，那么胎面+用户没有成员资格（Peloton不会告诉Recode其中有多少用户适合此类别）不会结束支付任何额外的费用。他们甚至可能会尽可能地决定自己的服务，他们会继续使用它，这对Peloton有益。如果需要的时间超过自由时期或根本不会发生，那么踏踏+所有者将不得不决定他们是否希望将设备保留为39美元或返回它。如果你是一个没有孩子或宠物的胎面+老板，可能会争辩说这种安全措施（和费用）并不需要，这太糟糕了。 
值得注意的是，踏板+已经带来了一个安全钥匙，即Peloton建议用户在没有使用机器时从儿童拆除和存储。这也值得注意，因为CPSC已经发生，而不是所有这些伤害都发生了因为孩子在没有人在周围时不小心转动机器。有些事情发生在父母使用机器时，以及世界上所有的安全钥匙和胎面锁都不会阻止它们。
 虽然Peloton显然有能力完全砖块，这将迫使业主归还它们，而是通过这种妥协，这是为了Peloton的利益。它也是其商业模式的一部分。机器本身是一次性购买，而订阅是一个连续的收入来源，是什么区别于传统机器的Peloton。这不仅仅是运动;它是一项服务的锻炼。因此，Peloton希望尽可能多的客户订阅。对情况的一个非常愤世嫉俗的情况会说这是这样做的一种方式。
 该更改仅似乎影响胎面+机器，而不是较小且更便宜的胎面模型，这是由于其触摸屏松开甚至脱落的伤害损伤报告的单独召回的主题。
 虽然这种情况的具体情况是不寻常的，但你没有完全拥有你购买的东西的想法不是。 Apple已使其设备越来越难以为任何人访问和修理 - 这也使Apple能够为该服务设定自己的价格 - 而John Deere类似地保持对运行其机器的软件的严格控制。您从技术上讲，您不拥有来自Apple Music或Amazon Prime的音乐，视频或您“购买”，并且他们可以从您身上带走。当Fitbit收购公司并关闭平台时，Pebble Smartwatches变得愚蠢（尽管扇形努力保持鹅卵石）。我们已经看到了大量保守的政治家和专家抱怨，社会媒体公司的转移服务条款已经让他们从言语或行动的平台启动，以便可以接受。
 Peloton一直非常成功，主要是由于它的“连接健身”模型，让客户每月继续支付，以获得其机器的充分利益。它还使Peloton更加连续地控制这些机器，而不是传统的，无连接的跑步机或运动自行车公司。然而，这种控制可以锻炼，或者软件和服务业务中的任何其他硬件制造商 - 希望它成为。
  数百万人转向Vox，了解新闻中发生的事情。我们的使命从未比这一刻更重要：通过了解来赋予。我们读者的财务贡献是支持我们资源密集型工作的关键部分，并帮助我们为所有人提供新闻。请考虑今天为vox提供贡献，从3美元的价格下降。
note_en:
Peloton has come up with an interesting solution to its treadmill safety issues, which have been connected to a child’s death: Force owners of its $4,295 Tread+ to either return the machine for a refund or pay a $39 monthly membership fee to use it at all. Users are outraged, and some have even compared Peloton’s demand  to ransomware.
 It’s a reminder that when you buy or use a product whose access is controlled by someone else, that access can always be taken away from you. As more and more of the things we buy are connected to the internet, living and dying by manufacturer-pushed software updates and on platforms that can be shuttered at any time, we have less and less control over them. Even if we pay a considerable amount of money for those devices, we may never fully own them.
 In this case, Peloton says the change was made for safety reasons, and that forcing customers into the membership was the only way to implement the update. Peloton  recalled the Tread+ in May, following the  death of a child who was pulled under a treadmill; reports of injuries to several other children, pets, and adults; and a  public spat with the Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC). The company stopped selling the Tread+ and urged Tread+ owners to “immediately stop” using them and offered full refunds for devices returned before November. But Peloton didn’t  completely discourage people from using the machine. In fact, it even offered to send movers out to owners’ homes to relocate their Tread+ units to a space that kids and pets couldn’t access, for free.
 Now, some Tread+ owners will face an extra monthly charge they didn’t have before. Peloton  recently informed them that soon they will no longer be able to use their machines unless they also purchase an All-Access membership for $39 a month. Peloton is offering three months of All-Access free to compensate for the inconvenience.
 Peloton told Recode the move was necessary to add a safety feature called “Tread Lock” to the machines.
 “In our ongoing work to ensure that our products are even safer and as part of our voluntary recall in collaboration with CPSC, we released Tread Lock, a four-digit passcode to secure Tread+ against unauthorized access,” Peloton told Recode. “Unfortunately, due to current technical limitations, Tread Lock is not yet available without a Peloton Membership.”
 Peloton told Recode that it’s working to make Tread Lock available for free but wouldn’t say how long it would take to implement the change. If it takes less than three months, then Tread+ users without a membership (Peloton wouldn’t tell Recode how many of its users fit into this category) won’t end up paying anything extra. They might even decide they like the service so much that they’ll keep using it, which is good for Peloton. If it takes longer than the free period or doesn’t happen at all, then Tread+ owners will have to decide if they want to keep the device and shell out $39 a month or return it. If you’re a Tread+ owner who doesn’t have kids or pets and might argue that this safety measure (and the expense) isn’t necessary, well, that’s just too bad.
 It’s worth noting that Tread+ already comes with a safety key that Peloton recommends users remove and store away from children when the machine isn’t being used. It’s also worth noting,  as the CPSC has, that not all of those injuries occurred because a child accidentally turned the machine on when no one was around. Some happened while a parent was using the machine, and all the safety keys and Tread Locks in the world wouldn’t have prevented them.
 While Peloton does apparently have the ability to brick its machines entirely, which would force owners to return them, it’s instead going with this compromise that is to Peloton’s benefit. It’s also part of its business model. The machine itself is a one-time purchase while subscriptions are a continuous source of revenue and what differentiates Peloton from traditional machines. It’s not just exercise; it’s  Exercise as a Service. So Peloton wants as many of its customers to subscribe as possible. A very cynical take on the situation would say that this is one way to do that.
 The change only appears to affect Tread+ machines and not the smaller and cheaper Tread model, which is the  subject of a separate recall due to reports of injuries from its touchscreen loosening and even falling off.
 While the specifics of this situation are unusual, the idea that you don’t entirely own the things you buy these days is not. Apple has made its devices  increasingly harder for anyone but Apple to access and repair — which also gives Apple the ability to set its own prices for that service — and John Deere similarly  maintains strict control over the software that runs its machines. You technically  don’t own the music, videos, or books you “purchase” from Apple Music or Amazon Prime, and they can be  taken away from you. Pebble smartwatches became dumb when Fitbit acquired the company and shut down the platform (although there have been fan-led efforts to  keep Pebbles going). And we’ve seen plenty of conservative politicians and pundits complain that social media companies’ shifting terms of service have gotten them booted from platforms for speech or actions that used to be acceptable.
 Peloton has been hugely successful, largely due to its “connected fitness” model that gets customers to keep paying every month to receive the full benefit of their machines. It also gives Peloton more continuous control over those machines than a traditional, non-connected treadmill or exercise bike company would ever have. And that control can be exercised however Peloton — or any other hardware manufacturer in the software and services business — wants it to be.

Windows 11 will have Microsoft Teams built in, in a bid to compete more directly with communication platforms like Apple’s FaceTime. And like FaceTime, the key here is cross-device integration, making the service more hardware-agnostic as people move from desktop to mobile and back again.
   In an era when we’ve got more video chat platforms than we know what to do with, it’s hard to shake the feeling that we might be seeing the final nail in the coffin for the once-mighty Skype, which the company bought way back in 2011 for $8.5 billion. As it tried to do with Skype, Microsoft is looking to blur the line between consumers and professionals with the platform.
 All versions of the new operating system will have Teams baked directly into the Start menu.

然而，除此之外，很明显，汽车公司将机器人视为未来的主要投资。真正对谈话充满了我的谈话，是三家公司 - 现代，福特和丰田的谈话方式 - 正在接近空间。如果没有别的，那是一个标志，这里有很多机会。
 我们在几个综述前福特在福特的野心中撰写了相当多的关于，包括与Mario Santillo进行采访，他加入了最近的小组。正如公司最近在密歇根大学投资所证明的那样，福特的方法在很大程度上是基于研究的。该公司希望在技术和人才方面进入地板。然而，当我按下Santillo关于收购时，（特别是因为它与数字制造商，敏捷）而言，他告诉我，“这总是我们正在看的东西。”所以不要统治它，特别是随着其他汽车公司开始在类别中锁定大名。
   当然，现代是最近记忆中最大的机器人学习之一，在SoftBank组合中的短暂上空之后拿起波士顿动力学（投资公司仍然是股东）。当然，除了公司之后的谷歌时间一直很重要，看到现货商业化和即将出售的延伸。 Hyundai的Ernestine Fu为我们在本周正式关闭的交易中提供更多光线，
 使用新的地平线工作室，当您将机器人与传统轮式运动相结合时，授权正在重新称解您可以做的事情，如行走机器人和步行车。显然，[波士顿动态]的技术在一起在使这些概念变得享有生命中的关键作用。
  正如我们所讨论的，丰田研究所在日本衰老人口类别中的兴趣。 EvercareCare构成了公司在机器人中的大部分内容的基础，包括一些家庭机器人研究，它本周展示了它的研究 - 特别是其成像如何处理反射和透明的表面。两者都传统上对机器人系统令人棘手。
   [I]日本，在20  -  30年内，超过65人的人数大致与65岁以下的人数大致相同。这将在劳动力方面具有一个非常有趣的社会经济影响。它可能会变得更大，我们在丰田在丰田正在寻找这些人们如何继续完成工作，因此他们可以让履行工作或在家里逗留更长。我们不想只是取代人民。我们真的想到我们如何保持以人为本和放大人民。
  我们现在一直遵循NVIDIA的ISAAC软件，机器人仿真软件可作为开放测试版提供。该软件建立在Omniverse之上，设计用于测试机器人的各种不同的相机和传感器功能。这是一个迷人的项目，并且可能是早期机器人初创公司的大量大量。 
快速跟进上周从机器人园林美化公司的一些资金，我要求Irobot与他们的Terra Mower发生了什么，这是延迟狂热相关的削减。该公司告诉我，无限延迟机器人仍然没有明确的时间表。
  与此同时，MISO机器人继续从Flippy中展开，汉堡翻转机器人具有自动饮料分配器。采用饮料分配器制造商Lancer创建的机器，完全自动化该过程，向下添加杯子上的盖子。然而，在产品中没有明确的时间表，但很有趣，可以在特定的部门中分配一些这些公司。
  一对仓库自动化故事，值得突出显示在这里。 Berkshire Gray推出了一堆新的履行机器人。本公司，
 这种新一代的移动机器人以更低的成本点提供更高的实现吞吐量，以实现更短的交货时间并支持更大数量的SKU。与固定输送带和早期发电机器人不同，伯克郡灰色的智能车队利用AI的力量来协调数千个移动机器人，以挑选，组织和提供各种客户和商店订单的项目。
  该公司将与每个机器人制造商同样地告诉您同一件事：这一切都是关于帮助零售商与亚马逊的庞然大物竞争。拟合，电子商务巨头本周也展示了一些新的机器人。亚马逊表示，自公司收购Kiva Systems以自亚马逊机器人翼组成的基础以来，它利用了350,000个移动驱动器单元。该职位导致一些人想知道，尽管有头部开始和一些大规模的资金，但公司是否开始落后于许多侵略性仓库机器人初创公司。
  本周不是很多资金新闻，但这应该很快改变。热机器人夏天几乎靠我们。我觉得它在空中。同时，请享受此潜水寄生虫，旨在追踪中间骨架“暮光之城”区域的广泛创造者。 Mesobot由树林洞海洋机构开发，旨在观察Zooplankton，凝胶状动物，而不会扰乱他们的栖息地。这是高级科学家Dana Yoerger，
 我们预计Mesobot将成为观察延长期间的重要工具，以及快速识别从容器生物体验中观察到的物种。因为美孚可以调查，追踪和录制引人注目的图像，我们希望透露以前未知的行为，物种相互作用，形态学结构和生物发光的使用。
note_en:
Beyond that, however, it’s clear that car companies see robotics as a key investment in their future. What really struck me about the conversation, is how differently the three companies – Hyundai, Ford and Toyota – are approaching the space. If nothing else, it’s a sign that there’s plenty of opportunity here.
 We  wrote quite a bit about Ford’s ambitions in the category several roundups ago, including an interview with Mario Santillo, who joined us for the recent panel. As evidenced by the company’s recent investment in University of Michigan, Ford’s approach is largely research-based. The company wants to be in on the ground floor both in terms of technology and talent. Though when I pressed Santillo about acquisitions, however, (specifically as it pertains to Digit-maker, Agility), he told me, “that’s always something we’re looking at.” So don’t rule it of, especially as other car companies begin to lockdown big names in the category.
   Hyundai, of course, made one of the biggest robotics acquisitions in recent memory, picking up Boston Dynamics after its short stint in the Softbank portfolio (the investment firm remains a shareholder). Of course, that post-Google time has been important for the company, seeing the commercialization of Spot and the  upcoming sale of Stretch. Hyundai’s Ernestine Fu shed some more light for us on the deal,  which officially closed this week,
 With New Horizon Studios, the mandate is reimagining what you can do when you combine robotics with traditional wheeled locomotion, like walking robots and walking vehicles. Obviously the technology that [Boston Dynamics] has put together plays a key role in enabling those sorts of concepts to come to life.
  As we discussed, the driving force in the Toyota Research Institute’s interest in the category Japan’s aging population. Eldercare forms the basis of much of what the company does in robotics, including some home robotics research that  it showed off this week – specifically how its imaging can handle reflective and transparent surfaces. Both have traditionally been tricky for robotic systems.
   [I]n Japan, in 20-30 years, the number of people who are over 65 will roughly be the same as the number of people who are under 65. That’s going to have a really interesting socioeconomic impact, in terms of the workforce. It’s probably going to be much older and we at Toyota are looking at how these people can keep doing their jobs, so they can get the fulfillment from doing their jobs or staying at home longer. We don’t want to just replace the people. We really think about how we stay human-centered and amplify people.
  We’ve been following  NVIDIA’s Isaac software for a while now, and the robotic simulation software  is available as an open beta. Built on top of Omniverse, the software is designed to test a wide range of different camera and sensor capabilities for robotics. It’s a fascinate project and potentially a big deal for early-stage robotics startups.
 Quick follow up to some funding last week from  a robots landscaping company, I asked iRobot what’s going on with their Terra mower, which was delayed amid pandemic-related cuts. The company tells me there’s still no clear timeline for the indefinitely delayed robot.
  Miso Robotics, meanwhile,  continues to build out from Flippy, the hamburger-flipping robot with an automated beverage dispenser. The machine, created with beverage dispenser manufacturer Lancer, fully automates the process, right down to adding the cap on the cup. No clear timeline on the product yet, however, but it’s fun to see some of these companies branch out in a given sector.
  A pair of warehouse automation stories worth highlighting here.  Berkshire Grey has unveiled a bunch of new fulfillment robots. Per the company,
 This new generation of mobile robots offers increased fulfillment throughput at a lower cost point to enable shorter delivery times and support a larger number of SKUs. Unlike fixed conveyor belts and early generation mobile robots, Berkshire Grey’s intelligent fleets harness the power of AI to orchestrate tens to thousands of mobile robots to pick, organize, and deliver items for a wide variety of customer and store orders.
  The company will tell you the same thing as every robotics maker: it’s all about helping retailers compete with the behemoth that is Amazon. Fittingly, the ecommerce giant also showed off  some new robots this week. Amazon says it’s utilized 350,000 mobile drive units since the company acquired Kiva Systems, which formed the foundation for its Amazon Robotics wing. The post has caused  some to wonder whether, in spite of a head start and some massive funding, whether the company is beginning to fall behind a number of aggressive warehouse robotics startups.
  Not a ton of funding news this week, but that should change soon. Hot bot summer is nearly upon us. I feel it in the air. Meantime,  please enjoy this submersible drone designed to track a wide range of creators in the mesopelagic “twilight” zone. Developed by the Woods Hole Oceanographic Institution,  Mesobot is designed to observe zooplankton, gelatinous animals and more, without disturbing their habitat. Here’s Senior Scientist Dana Yoerger,
 We expect that Mesobot will emerge as a vital tool for observing midwater organisms for extended periods, as well as rapidly identifying species observed from vessel biosonars. Because Mesobot can survey, track, and record compelling imagery, we hope to reveal previously unknown behaviors, species interactions, morphological structures, and the use of bioluminescence.

Chuck Phillips是Meetwell的联合创始人，这是一个能够使团队能够实现更好的工作生活平衡的应用程序，同时通过减少他们参加的糟糕会议的金额来储蓄公司。
  繁忙的会议组织者驾驶 - 通过时间安排会议，因为他们认为他们没有时间计划。他们希望从少量准备中获得良好的结果，不会发生这种情况。会议正在举行，进步踩踏。
 拯救每个人的一种方法（并赢得很多朋友）将是为了摆脱所有会议，但是一场精心准备和经营良好的会议可以加快沟通，并让团队更接近其目标。不幸的是，大多数会议都是懒洋洋地策划，跑步，监禁与会者并停止生产力。
   没有人衡量他们的会议的影响。因此，第一步是开始保持会议指标，以便您可以识别您的团队日历中的糟糕会议。
  我公司创建了一位日历助理，在发生之前，自动衡量并停止差别会议，但如果您无法自动化在会议结束后从与会者预防会议，调查和学习，以便记录和衡量它们。
 创建分类法并量化正在举行的会议类型 - 例如：“信息共享”，“头脑风暴”，“1：1”，“决策”等。
 经过几个月（理想情况地是一年）收集指标，您可以评估质量并寻找模式。你可能会发现这些线条的东西： 
很少有员工拒绝会议，即使是显而易见的是，会议将是一个多泽的。
note_en:
Chuck Phillips is the co-founder of  MeetWell, an application that enables teams to achieve a better work-life balance while saving companies money by reducing the amount of bad meetings they attend.
  Busy meeting organizers drive-thru schedule meetings because they think they don’t have time to plan. They expect good outcomes to come from little preparation, which doesn’t happen. The meetings are being held and progress is stilted.
 One way to save everyone significant time (and win lots of friends) would be to just get rid of all meetings, but a well-prepared and well-run session can expedite communication and get a team closer to its goals. Unfortunately, most meetings are  lazily planned and poorly run, imprisoning attendees and halting productivity.
   No one measures the impact of their meetings. So the first step is to start keeping meeting metrics so that you can identify the bad meetings on your teams’ calendars.
  My company has created  a calendar assistant that automatically measures and stops bad meetings before they occur, but if you can’t automate the prevention of bad meetings, survey and learn from attendees after the meeting to record and measure them.
 Create taxonomies and quantify the types of meetings that are being held — for example: “information sharing,” “brainstorming,” “1:1,” “decision-making,” etc.
 After several months (ideally a year) of collecting metrics, you can grade the quality and look for patterns. You will probably find something along these lines:
 Very few employees decline meetings, even when it’s obvious that the meeting is going to be a doozy.

 Ryte首席执行官Andy Bruckschloegl表示：“目前有1.89亿有活跃的网站。我们的目标是占所有活跃网站的5％，或950万个网站，在我们的平台，强大的合作伙伴，社交媒体活动的帮助下，2023年底，气候中立的气候中立。时间是滴答作响，并使网站碳中性与其他行业和流程相比真正简单。“
 Ryte表示，它还与尼加拉瓜圣何塞的重新造林项目合作，让客户通过购买气候证书来抵消其剩余的排放。 
使用专有算法，Ryte表示它测量整个网站的代码，平均页面大小，以及通道的每月流量，然后产生它使用的CO2量的计算。  允许有类似的服务，但这些是ad-hoc，没有连接到平台。 简单的谷歌搜索将带来WebsiteCarbon，Ecosistant和学术论文等网站。 但据我所知，这样的初创公司还没有将这种服务放入他们的平台上。  “与Ryte合作将有助于提高信息技术如何促进气候变化的认识 - 同时提供有关工具的工具。 Ryte的行业领先的碳计算器使成千上万的网站所有者能够了解他们的碳足迹，以抵消不可避免的碳排放，从而为综合气候行动战略奠定基础，“Tristan A. Foerster，Co-Ceo ClimatePartner。
note_en:
It transpires that a new service has identified how to gauge that, and now it’s raised Venture capital to scale.
 Ryte raised €8.5 million ($10M) in a previously undisclosed round led by Bayern Kapital out of Munich and Octopus Investments out of London earlier this year for its Website User Experience Platform.
  It has now launched the ‘Ryte Website Carbon KPI’, which claims to be able to help make 5% of all websites carbon neutral by 2023.
 Ryte says it worked with data scientists and environmental experts to develop the ability to accurately measure the carbon impact of client’s websites. According to carbon transition thinktank, the Shift Project, the carbon footprint of our gadgets, the internet, and the systems supporting them accounts for about 3.7% of global greenhouse emissions. And this trend is rising rapidly as the world digitizes itself, especially post-pandemic.
 Ryte has now engaged its Data Scientist, Katharina Meraner, who has a PhD in climate science and global warming, and input from Climate Partner, to launch this new service.
 Andy Bruckschloegl, CEO of Ryte said: “There are currently 189 million active websites. Our goal is to make 5% of all active websites, or 9.5 million websites, climate neutral by the end of 2023 with the help of our platform, strong partners, social media activities, and much more. Time is ticking and making websites carbon neutral is really easy compared to other industries and processes.”
 Ryte says it is also collaborating with a reforestation project in San Jose, Nicaragua, to allow its customers to offset their remaining emissions through the purchase of climate certificates.
 Using a proprietary algorithm, Ryte says it measures the code of the entire website, average page size, as well as monthly traffic by channel then produces a calculation of the amount of CO2 it uses up.
 Admittedly there are similar services but these are ad-hoc and not connected to a platform. A simple Google search will bring us sites like  Websitecarbon,  Ecosistant, and academic papers. But as far as I can tell, a startup like this hasn’t put this kind of service into their platform yet.
 “Teaming up with Ryte will help raise awareness on how information technology contributes to climate change – while at the same time providing tools to make a difference. Ryte’s industry-leading carbon calculator enables thousands of website owners to understand their carbon footprint, to offset unavoidable carbon emissions and thus lay a basis for a comprehensive climate action strategy,” commented Tristan A. Foerster, Co-CEO ClimatePartner.
title: 在筹集1000万美元后，Ryte推出了“碳KPI”来测量网站的CO2占地面积
title_en: After raising $10M, Ryte launches ‘Carbon KPI’ to measure the CO2 footprint of web sites
